Question: Expand $$\log_{3} ( \frac{7 x^{2} + 21 x}{7 x ( x - 1 ) ( x - 2 )} ) .$$
o3 Answer:
Expand the logarithm by applying the quotient, product, and power rules step-by-step.

1.  Separate the numerator and denominator (quotient rule):
   log₃[(7x² + 21x)/(7x(x – 1)(x – 2))]  
   = log₃(7x² + 21x) – log₃[7x(x – 1)(x – 2)].

2.  Factor each polynomial so the product rule can be used easily.

   •  Numerator: 7x² + 21x = 7x(x + 3).  
   •  Denominator is already a product: 7x(x – 1)(x – 2).

3.  Expand each logarithm into a sum (product rule):

   Numerator  
   log₃(7x² + 21x) = log₃[7x(x + 3)]  
    = log₃7 + log₃x + log₃(x + 3).

   Denominator  
   log₃[7x(x – 1)(x – 2)]  
    = log₃7 + log₃x + log₃(x – 1) + log₃(x – 2).

4.  Subtract the two results and cancel common terms:

   log₃(7x² + 21x) – log₃[7x(x – 1)(x – 2)]  
   = (log₃7 + log₃x + log₃(x + 3))  
     – (log₃7 + log₃x + log₃(x – 1) + log₃(x – 2))  
   = log₃(x + 3) – log₃(x – 1) – log₃(x – 2).

Therefore the expanded form is

log₃(x + 3) – log₃(x – 1) – log₃(x – 2),

with the implied domain x > 2 (so each logarithm’s argument is positive).
